paper_metadata.title: Solving Quantitative Reasoning Problems with Language Models
paper_metadata.abstract: Language models have achieved remarkable performance on a wide range of tasks that require natural language understanding. Nevertheless, state-of-the-art models have generally struggled with tasks that require quantitative reasoning, such as solving mathematics, science, and engineering problems at the college level. To help close this gap, we introduce Minerva, a large language model pretrained on general natural language data and further trained on technical content. The model achieves state-of-the-art performance on technical benchmarks without the use of external tools. We also evaluate our model on over two hundred undergraduate-level problems in physics, biology, chemistry, economics, and other sciences that require quantitative reasoning, and find that the model can correctly answer nearly a third of them.

extraction_data.innovation: Large-scale language models trained on diverse text data for general language tasks.
extraction_data.application: Chat, instruction following, general language tasks

extraction_data.innovation: Large-scale language model trained on a diverse corpus for solving quantitative reasoning problems, demonstrating improved performance on math and reasoning tasks.
extraction_data.extension: The model uses a Transformer-based architecture with a focus on solving quantitative reasoning problems, including geometric and arithmetic sequences, probability, and logical reasoning.
extraction_data.research_problem: Improving language models for quantitative reasoning and logical problem-solving.
extraction_data.application: Quantitative reasoning, logical problem-solving, and general language tasks.
extraction_data.paper_title: Solving Quantitative Reasoning Problems with Language Models

extraction_data.innovation: Improved quantitative reasoning capabilities through larger model size and fine-tuning on diverse math and reasoning tasks, addressing errors in smaller models such as incorrect probability calculations and arithmetic sequence reasoning.
extraction_data.extension: The 540B model corrects errors in smaller models, such as calculating probabilities correctly and solving arithmetic sequences accurately, demonstrating improved reasoning capabilities.
extraction_data.research_problem: Improving language models for quantitative reasoning and logical problem-solving.
extraction_data.application: Quantitative reasoning, logical problem-solving, and general language tasks.
extraction_data.paper_title: Solving Quantitative Reasoning Problems with Language Models
